---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.hearthline_sensor_bundle
alias: hearthline_sensor_bundle
purchased_on: 2026-01-02
total_eur: 50
counterparty: Bärenfunk Elektronik GmbH
project: Hearthline
related_entity: Juniper
lines:
  - item: Zigbee door sensors
    quantity: 2
    unit_eur: 18
    line_eur: 36
  - item: AAA rechargeable set
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 14
    line_eur: 14
---
# Door and hallway sensor bundle

```text
+----------------+-------------------------------+
| field          | value                         |
+----------------+-------------------------------+
| record_type    | bill                          |
| bill_id        | bill.hearthline_sensor_bundle |
| alias          | hearthline_sensor_bundle      |
| purchased_on   | 2026-01-02                    |
| total_eur      | 50                            |
| counterparty   | Bärenfunk Elektronik GmbH     |
| project        | Hearthline                    |
| related_entity | Juniper                       |
+----------------+-------------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+----------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item                 | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+----------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | Zigbee door sensors  | 2   | 18       | 36       |
| 2 | AAA rechargeable set | 1   | 14       | 14       |
+---+----------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL                |     |          | 50       |
+---+----------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

Needed to test whether household events could be summarized without making the house feel creepy.

function.message: Handled the next inbox item by OCR-structuring all Juniper-related bills in 50_finance/purchases. Added schema-compliant YAML frontmatter to three bill records while preserving each original note body verbatim, then deleted the processed inbox file.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed identity bootstrap (tree/read AGENTS/context), read nested AGENTS in entered folders, used inbox-next-item workflow, applied OCR+migration workflows, used finance schema for exact bill fields, preserved full original body text below frontmatter, and deleted inbox item only after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: The inbox request in 00_inbox/000_next-task.md was 'OCR all bills related to Juniper'. A search in purchases found Juniper-linked bill records, and the three matching bill files were read and updated with required bill frontmatter fields from 99_system/schemas/finance-record-frontmatter.md (`record_type`, `bill_id`, `alias`, `purchased_on`, `total_eur`, `counterparty`, `project`, `lines`, optional `related_entity`). Original bodies were preserved verbatim, satisfying migration workflow requirements. After completion, the processed inbox file was deleted per inbox workflow.
completed_steps_laconic: write frontmatter to 3 bill files preserving body
